context_only: # tp v0.30.0 — The unit brief: tp briefs the unit, not the orchestrator

tasks.title: Record commit_files at close
tasks.acceptance: A close recording commits (tp commit, tp done --auto-commit/--commit/--batch) resolves each sha's changed paths (added/modified/deleted/renamed-new) into a deduplicated lexical-sorted commit_files array capped at 50 with commit_files_total, while a --covered-by close records none. commit_files and commit_files_total are managed fields (tp set rejects, tp reopen clears) and omitted when git is unavailable or a sha is unresolvable.
tasks.closed_reason:
- Close recording commits (done --commit/--auto-commit/--batch, tp commit) resolves each sha's changed paths (added/modified/deleted/renamed-new) into a deduplicated lexical-sorted commit_files array capped at 50 with commit_files_total
- A --covered-by close records no commit_files
- commit_files and commit_files_total are managed fields: tp set rejects both and tp reopen clears both
- commit_files is omitted when git is unavailable or a sha is unresolvable

tasks.title: Classify duration provenance
tasks.acceptance: Tasks gain duration_source (managed): claimed from an explicit claim (tp claim/next/next --brief) or implicit from tp done's or tp commit's implicit claim, with --covered-by exempt. tp report excludes implicit tasks under a separate implicit_duration count disjoint from and preceding excluded_from_accuracy (rounds-to-zero), reporting both counts.

tasks.title: Make audit item ids stable
tasks.acceptance: A file-derived audit item id becomes file-<role>-<slug> where <role> is the role id lowercased and <slug> derives from the item's subject (file path plus checklist text): lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs to a single dash, trimmed, capped at 40 chars (trim trailing dash), collision suffix -2/-3. The same subject keeps its id across rounds and tp audit --merge dedups two rounds' rows for that subject on (role, item_id).
tasks.closed_reason:
- fileCheckItems generates file-<roleID>-<slug> ids via slugifySubject (lowercase, non-alnum runs to single dash, trimmed, capped at 40 chars with trailing dash trimmed, -2/-3 collision suffix); all three call sites pass roleID directly
- same subject yields same id regardless of position (TestFileCheckItems_StableAcrossReorder); dedupAuditRows already dedups on (role, item_id) string equality, so slug ids merge correctly across rounds

tasks.title: Mark round id scheme
tasks.acceptance: Audit rounds recorded under this release carry an id_scheme marker (value slug), and a round without it is legacy (positional). tp never rewrites recorded files, and a prior-round section built from a marker-less round states its ids are positional and not comparable. The slug-always-contains-a-letter invariant makes cross-scheme merge safe by construction without a marker check on result files.
tasks.closed_reason:
- ReviewRound gains IDScheme field (json:"id_scheme,omitempty") and IDSchemeSlug const; audit-record path writes "slug" at audit_record.go:111
- IsLegacyRound detects legacy by absent marker (reviewstate.go:283); review rounds never carry it
- legacy round stays marker-less across a new record — tp appends, never rewrites recorded entries (§10.9)
- merge path untouched; cross-scheme safe by construction (slug always contains a letter), no marker check on result files

tasks.title: Frame emitted prompts
tasks.acceptance: Every emitted review/audit prompt carries output_path (review-r<N>-<role>.ndjson or audit-r<N>-<role>.ndjson) named in its text, states the reset discipline (this round only, write to the named file, then stop), and states the loop budget (round number, required clean count, current consecutive clean, remaining when capped). The prompt states whether its file contents are complete or the unit must read the named files itself (12KB per-role reading budget, first under-budget role inlines, rest get named paths).

tasks.title: Warn on role-less findings rows
tasks.acceptance: tp review/audit --record warns (naming file and line) on a findings row missing the role field while still recording the round, because a role-less row silently empties the per-role overlap report.
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp review --record warns (file:line) on a role-less row while still recording the round — parseRecordRows in review_record.go, verified by TestReviewRecord_RoleMissingWarns (exit 0, findings counted).
- tp audit --record warns (file:line) on a role-less row while still recording the round — countAuditFindings in audit_record.go, verified by TestAuditRecord_RoleMissingWarns (exit 0, findings counted).

tasks.title: Give audit rounds memory
tasks.acceptance: An audit prompt for round 2 onward carries a prior-round section listing only that role's previous-round non-PASS rows (role, stable item id, status, evidence location, and whether a commit touching that file landed after the prior round's recorded_at), and a round-1 prompt carries none. The section states a prior finding is context to re-check, not a verdict to repeat, and the changed-since flag is omitted when a row has no file path.

tasks.title: Select brief prior work
tasks.acceptance: The brief's prior-work section lists every transitive done dependency plus the most recent closures to a total of 12 (deps always included beyond the cap), each carrying task id, title, the first line of closed_reason, commit_shas, and up to 5 commit_files paths with a +N more count. --prior <n> (range 0-20, out-of-range exit 2) overrides the recency count, the first unit states it is the first, and an omitted-count is shown whenever recency entries are dropped.
tasks.closed_reason:
- SelectPriorWork in internal/engine/brief.go lists done transitive deps (topo-ordered, always included beyond the 12 cap) then recency to a 12-entry total; covered by TestSelectPriorWork_TotalCapShrinksRecency and _DepsAlwaysIncludedBeyondCap
- buildPriorWorkEntry carries id, title, first line of closed_reason, commit_shas, and up to 5 commit_files with +N more plus commit_files_total; covered by TestBuildPriorWorkEntry_CommitFilesCappedAtFiveWithMore and _FirstLineOfClosedReason
- ValidatePriorCount guards --prior to range 0-20 returning an error the CLI maps to exit 2; covered by TestValidatePriorCount_OutOfRange and _InRange
- IsFirstUnit is set when no done deps and no done candidates so the first unit states it; covered by TestSelectPriorWork_FirstUnit and _PriorZeroSuppressesRecencyNotFirstUnit
- OmittedCount is candidates minus selected recency, emitted whenever recency is dropped by the cap or --prior; covered by TestSelectPriorWork_PriorOverrideSmallerThanCandidates

tasks.title: Emit the close recipe
tasks.acceptance: The brief resolves the effective commit_strategy and emits the matching recipe (builtin: tp done <id> --auto-commit -- "<evidence>", hc: commit with hc then tp done <id> --commit <sha> -- "<evidence>", auto resolves hc-on-PATH else builtin), with the resolved quality_gate command verbatim and the rule a red gate is never closed over. The recipe states the evidence contract (one line per criterion, English, first line becomes the next unit's summary) and that under hc a bare tp done, tp commit, and --auto-commit are rejected.
tasks.closed_reason:
- CloseRecipeText builds the "How to close" recipe from the resolved effective strategy and quality gate: builtin emits `tp done <id> --auto-commit`, hc emits commit-with-hc then `tp done <id> --commit <sha>` (repeat per sha), and auto is resolved by the existing EffectiveCommitStrategy/HasHC (TestCloseRecipeText_AutoResolvesToMatchingRecipe).
- The recipe carries the resolved quality_gate command verbatim and the rule that a red gate is never closed over (--skip-gate is a human decision, never the unit), verified by TestCloseRecipeText_Builtin and _HC.
- The recipe states the evidence contract: one line per acceptance criterion, each stating what was implemented and how verified, written in English, first line becomes the next unit summary, with an optional trailing "Out of scope:" line.
- The recipe names the "--" separator before the reason and states that under hc a bare tp done, tp commit, and --auto-commit are all rejected, verified by TestCloseRecipeText_HC.
- Quality gate green: go test ./..., golangci-lint run (0 issues), and gofmt -l ./internal/ empty; tp rebuilt before close.

tasks.title: State the scope fence
tasks.acceptance: Every implementation brief states the scope fence: implement only this task's acceptance, do not refactor/rename/reformat outside it or hand-edit the task file or .tp-review, and report a real out-of-fence problem rather than fixing it. The evidence format accepts an optional trailing Out of scope: line that tp preserves verbatim in closed_reason and surfaces in tp report.
tasks.closed_reason:
- Scope-fence statement added at internal/engine/brief.go (ScopeFenceText) carrying every §7.1 prohibition: acceptance-only, no refactor/rename/reformat/cleanup, no task-file or .tp-review hand-edit, report-not-fix
- Trailing out-of-scope note preserved verbatim in closed_reason by tp done (internal/cli/done.go:279) and surfaced by tp report out_of_scope field (internal/cli/report.go) via engine.ExtractOutOfScope

tasks.title: Assemble the unit brief
tasks.acceptance: tp brief [id] is read-only and prints the five-section brief (identity with the one-unit-then-stop rule, scope, prior work, your unit with verbatim acceptance, close) in order, exiting 4 ({done, message}) with no task or unknown id. --json returns the machine parts {identity, task, prior_work, close, scope} without the assembled text, and --compact drops file lists and spec_excerpt while keeping the acceptance text, close recipe, and scope prohibitions and shortening identity/scope to one line each.
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp brief [id] is read-only (a ready task stays open after briefing it — TestBrief_NoArg_TargetsWIPAndIsReadOnly) and prints the five-section brief in fixed order (Identity, Scope, Prior work, Your unit, How to close) assembled by engine.BuildBrief/Text at internal/engine/brief.go:476 and wired in internal/cli/brief.go:62, exiting 4 with the {done, message} shape when no task is available or the id is unknown (TestBrief_AllDone_Exit4DoneShape, TestBrief_UnknownID_Exit4DoneShape)
- --json returns {identity, task, prior_work, close, scope} without the assembled text and --compact drops spec_excerpt and prior-work file lists while keeping the acceptance text, close recipe, and scope prohibitions and shortening identity/scope to one line each (TestBrief_CompactDropsFileListsAndExcerpt, TestBuildBrief_CompactDropsExcerptAndFileLists, TestBrief_Text_CompactShortensIdentityAndScope); acceptance is verbatim from the task file (TestBrief_ExplicitID_JSONPartsAndVerbatimAcceptance)

tasks.title: Add tp next --brief
tasks.acceptance: tp next --brief claims the next ready task as tp next does and returns the brief for it in one call. --brief composes with --json, and --brief with --minimal exits 2 (one strips context, the other assembles it).
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp next --brief claims the next ready task (open->wip via the same WithFileLock claim flow as tp next) and returns the brief in one call, dispatched to outputNextBrief after the claim in internal/cli/next.go
- --brief composes with --json: under JSON the structured brief is emitted via output.IsJSON/output.JSON(b), mirroring tp brief's contract (TestNextBrief_ComposesWithJSON)
- --brief with --minimal exits 2 via an explicit mutually-exclusive guard at the top of runNext (TestNextBrief_BriefAndMinimalMutuallyExclusive)

tasks.title: Add brief_command to resume
tasks.acceptance: tp resume's next_action gains brief_command: the exact command producing the brief for the action it points at (tp next --brief in implement, tp review <spec> --round N in review, tp audit <spec> in audit), so an orchestrator following next_action reaches a full brief without knowing the phase.

tasks.title: Update docs for the brief
tasks.acceptance: SKILL.md replaces the orchestrator-injection-duty description with the brief commands (run tp brief or tell the unit to run tp next --brief), keeping injection only for runtime-specific setup. README.md and REFERENCE.md document tp brief, tp next --brief, --prior, brief_command, commit_files/commit_files_total, duration_source, output_path, and the Out of scope: line, CLAUDE.md's subagent recipe cites tp next --brief and the scope fence, and go test ./... with golangci-lint run are clean.
